title: Sophronitis liliputana
intro:
I couldn't find any information on Sophronitis liliputana, but I believe you may be referring to Sophronitis (now known as Zygopetalum), Liliputiana is another name for one of its species (Zygopetalum liliputianum). 

However, I found some information on Zygopetalum liliputianum. Here's a paragraph about it:

Zygopetalum liliputianum is a terrestrial orchid species, characterized by its small size and delicate white flowers. The health benefits of Zygopetalum liliputianum are largely anecdotal, but it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Zygopetalum liliputianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Zygopetalum liliputianum include teas and tinctures, which are used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Sophronitis liliputana is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Further classification places it within the subfamily Cymbidieae. Within this subfamily, Sophronitis liliputana is part of the tribe Maxillarieae. It is also known to be a synonym for the genus Brachystele.
preparations: Sophronitis liliputana is a species of orchid, but I couldn't find any information on herbal preparations specifically related to this plant.
